mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly increasing present value of future corporate cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2024-07-01 suggests potential easing cycle ahead
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05 vs. baseline).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a 2-month window, signaling labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice recession probability.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% as of 2024-07-01, near historical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or Rank IC of UNRATE change vs. 5-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate (measured quarterly), indicating weakening aggregate demand that compresses earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate falls below 2% annualized
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with slowing growth momentum
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances following GDPC1 deceleration below 2%, or Rank IC of GDPC1 growth vs. 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both decline simultaneously by more than 5% month-over-month, indicating synchronized contraction in consumer and residential demand that signals imminent earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 5% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 698835.0M (revised) and HOUST at 1327.0K (revised) as of 2024-06-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 5% simultaneously, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) rises above its 12-month moving average while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable below 3% year-over-year, indicating healthy supply-side expansion without overheating that supports positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved profit margins.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 12-month MA and CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 3%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.9757 (revised) and CPIAUCSL at 313.569 (revised) as of 2024-07-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances where INDPRO > 12-month MA and CPIAUCSL YoY < 3%, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month over a 3-month rolling average, signaling labor market weakening that reduces consumer spending power and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth rate falls below 100K jobs per month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where PAYEMS 3-month growth < 100K, or Rank IC of PAYEMS growth vs. 20-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) declines month-over-month, indicating monetary policy is restrictive relative to inflation trajectory, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as real rates rise.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% and CPIAUCSL at 313.569 as of 2024-07-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 5.0% and CPIAUCSL declines MoM, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1.4 million units annualized while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains above 2% annualized, indicating robust residential demand and economic expansion that supports positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through sustained earnings growth and multiplier effects.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1.4M annualized and GDPC1 QoQ growth > 2% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1327.0K (revised) and GDPC1 at 23286.508B (revised) as of 2024-06-01 and 2024-04-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances where HOUST > 1.4M and GDPC1 QoQ > 2%, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
